text:
## B45 - split-level L-plan OCBF
Realistic 3D steel building B45: split-level L-plan OCBF; office wing 4 storeys (roof 50 ft) + workshop wing 2 tall storeys (roof 36 ft) with NO aligned floor elevations and NO seismic joint; shared grid-line columns take connections at 6 elevations. Lateral system distributed OCBF (chevron + single-diagonal bays, stiffness-equivalent diagonals); all bases pinned. SDC C, R 3.25, Ie 1.0; wind checked per wing. Plan L-shaped (re-entrant). MODELING RECIPE for the split level: merge the two near-coincident roof/floor diaphragms (24 in offset) into ONE diaphragm at the mean elevation with a designed step shear-transfer detail along the shared line, OR keep both levels and declare cfg['drift_exempt_stories'] for the offset story -- never leave a phantom 24-in story in the drift gate. Loads per wing via D_by_level/L_by_level; drift snow at the 14-ft step. Results: T1 = 0.48 s; base shear 317 kip; max inter-story drift 0.46%. All engineering sanity checks pass. Sections (designed): braces HSS6X6X3/8-HSS7X7X1/2, columns W12, girders W21X62/W24X84. Validated 3D model; units kip-inch; analyses ['ELF']; OCBF system.

Loads per wing via D_by_level/L_by_level; drift snow at the 14-ft step. Results: T1 = 0.48 s; base shear 317 kip; max inter-story drift 0.46%. All engineering sanity checks pass. text:
## B46 - L-plan stepped-roof EBF (shear links)
Realistic 3D steel building B46: L-plan EBF, 8-storey north bar + 5-storey west leg (stepped roof), re-entrant Type 2 -> MRSA + 25% diaphragm/collector increase on the re-entrant lines. Lateral system distributed EBF bays (perimeter + re-entrant lines); links e = 36 in, shear-governed (e <= 1.6Mp/Vp verified); elastic idealization: model each EBF bay as a full-length continuous beam + single diagonal brace (the validated-library convention), then derive link shear as Vlink = Vbay*h/(L-e) and run the AISC 341 F3.3 capacity chain (1.25RyVn; 0.88x beams outside links) by hand. All bases pinned. SDC D, R 8, Cd 4, Ie 1.0. Results: T1 = 1.17 s; base shear 876 kip; max inter-story drift 0.83%; link rotation 0.062 rad <= 0.08. All engineering sanity checks pass. Sections (designed): links W24X94, braces HSS10X10X5/8, EBF columns W14X398-W14X109. Validated 3D model; units kip-inch; analyses ['ELF','RS']; EBF system.

text:
## B47 - wedding-cake dual SMF+BRBF tower
Realistic 3D steel building B47: 20-storey dual system (perimeter SMF + continuous BRBF chevron core), TWO asymmetric setbacks (tiers 7/14/20, 255.5 ft) - vertical Type 3 at both setbacks; setback terrace transfer collectors with Omega0; SMF verified to carry >= 25% of V at every level on a brace-removed model. BRB chevron bays modeled as stiffness-equivalent diagonals (KF~1.45 x Asc); adjusted strengths beta*omega*Ry*Pysc drive beams/columns. NOTE the honesty tell: at this height the largest rolled W14 can be INADEQUATE for the core-base overturning axial - flag it and use a built-up box (BU30x30x2.5) rather than shipping D/C > 1. Bases fixed (SMF+BRBF). SDC D, R 8, Cd 5, Ie 1.0. Results: T1 = 2.70 s; base shear 1728 kip; max inter-story drift 1.99%. All engineering sanity checks pass. Validated 3D model; units kip-inch; analyses ['ELF','RS']; dual SMF+BRBF system.

BRB chevron bays modeled as stiffness-equivalent diagonals (KF~1.45 x Asc); adjusted strengths beta*omega*Ry*Pysc drive beams/columns. NOTE the honesty tell: at this height the largest rolled W14 can be INADEQUATE for the core-base overturning axial - flag it and use a built-up box (BU30x30x2.5) rather than shipping D/C > 1. Bases fixed (SMF+BRBF). SDC D, R 8, Cd 5, Ie 1.0. Results: T1 = 2.70 s; base shear 1728 kip; max inter-story drift 1.99%. All engineering sanity checks pass. Validated 3D model; units kip-inch; analyses ['ELF','RS']; dual SMF+BRBF system.

text:
## B48 - offset-core SCBF (torsionally irregular)
Realistic 3D steel building B48: 9-storey + partial 10th, notched rectangular plan with ALL braced bays clustered in an OFFSET end core - torsionally irregular BY DESIGN. Two-story-X SCBF bays modeled with BOTH diagonals per story. THE LESSON: compute the rigid-diaphragm torsion ratio delta_max/delta_avg at the plan extremes FIRST - with every frame near the centre of rigidity the as-drawn layout gives ratio ~2+ (extreme Type 1b) and a torsional fundamental mode (T1 ~ 5 s, drift off-scale); it is NOT buildable without a supplemental braced bay at the far end (documented deviation), after which ratio ~1.24 (Type 1a) with Ax amplification and rho = 1.3. 145-ft gravity-only zone dragged to the core by Omega0 collectors. SDC D, R 6, Cd 5, Ie 1.0. Results (with the supplemental bay): T1 = 1.06 s; base shear 1209 kip; max inter-story drift 0.88%. All engineering sanity checks pass. Validated 3D model; units kip-inch; analyses ['ELF','RS']; SCBF system.
